methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $22M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
RISE Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Arron Acosta and Blake Sessions that has raised $22M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private robotics firms at Series B stage and 1 public company, reflecting the nascent nature of the sector.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis uses the $22M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $44M. Peer-median analysis references 6 same-sector, same-to-adjacent-stage companies: 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), Fairmat ($350M), and one additional peer. The median valuation across this set is $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B.

The triangulated analysis suggests RISE Robotics supports a valuation range of $37M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $560M. The wide dispersion reflects substantial uncertainty endemic to early-stage robotics valuations, where peer outcomes range across two orders of magnitude. Stage-progression and peer-median methods yield materially different results, indicating that Stage A positioning and comparables-based approaches assign substantially different weight to company potential. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to later-stage funded peers and path-to-revenue trajectory in a capital-intensive sector.
